items.title: Suspected gunman at Washington press dinner identified as 31-year-old Californian
items.summary: The man arrested at the event attended by President Trump has been named in US media as Cole Tomas Allen from California.

items.title: Oil prices rise as US-Iran peace talks stall
items.summary: President Trump said the US had cancelled plans to send a team to Pakistan for negotiations.

items.title: Ukraine's drone commander has Russian oil, troops and morale in his sights
items.summary: In a rare interview, Commander Robert Brovdi shared how his unit accounts for a third of all targets destroyed on the battlefield.

items.title: At least 42 killed in Chad after water well dispute escalates
items.summary: The dispute was initially between two families before escalating into a cycle of reprisal attacks.

items.title: Michael Jackson biopic smashes box office record
items.summary: The film received poor reviews but has stormed the box office, taking $217m (£160m) globally.

items.title: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
items.summary: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

items.title: Pirates seize another vessel off Somali coast as threat level increased
items.summary: The UK's maritime monitor reports at least four suspected piracy incidents in the past week off the coast of Somalia.

items.title: Trump says King will be 'very safe' during US visit after security talks
items.summary: The state visit will go ahead despite concerns raised after a gunman targeted an event attended by the president.

items.title: Missing 5-year-old girl likely abducted from Outback home, police say
items.summary: Police are searching for a man recently released from prison over her disappearance.

items.title: French teen who licked vending machine straw faces jail in Singapore
items.summary: The teen, who posted on social media a video of himself licking the straw, could be jailed for more than two years.

items.title: Death toll in Colombia highway bus bomb attack rises to 20
items.summary: Political violence has escalated in Colombia ahead of next month's presidential election.

items.title: Mali defence minister killed as country hit by wave of rebel attacks
items.summary: A wave of coordinated attacks by jihadist militants and separatists has spread through the country.

items.title: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
items.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

items.title: The Chinese sports brand taking on Nike and Adidas
items.summary: Now one of the biggest sportswear firms, Anta's rise follows a playbook adopted by many Chinese giants.

items.title: Canada's Carney has enjoyed a long political honeymoon. Now comes the test
items.summary: Global acclaim and domestic political gains have defined Mark Carney's first year as Canada's PM. He now faces pressure to deliver on his bold promises.

items.title: Remembering Raghu Rai: The photographer who showed India to itself
items.summary: With Rai's passing, India has lost a visual historian who was a chronicler of its most defining and often painful moments.

items.title: Sawe smashes two-hour mark to 'move goalposts for marathon running'
items.summary: Sabastian Sawe makes history at the London Marathon as the first person to run a sub-two-hour marathon in a competitive race.

items.title: 'We cried together': Trump's deportation drive forces tough decisions for couples
items.summary: The spouses of illegal immigrants facing deportation must choose between separation or moving to a new country.

items.title: Heated Rivalry, The Housemaid and Fourth Wing: TikTok launches BookTok bestseller list
items.summary: The inaugural top 20 is entirely made up of female authors, with Chloe Walsh appearing most frequently.

items.title: Did Trump save eight Iranian women from execution?
items.summary: The US president says he saved eight Iranian women from execution, but Iran says otherwise.

items.title: Video shows correspondents' dinner suspect charge checkpoint
items.summary: Surveillance video released by Donald Trump shows the suspect running past metal detectors as security agents draw their guns.

items.title: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
items.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

items.title: Watch: How one orangutan braved new bridge to unite his split community
items.summary: The forest where the Sumatran orangutans live has been split by a road.

items.title: Hope in Gaza as 300 couples get married at one time
items.summary: The 300 couples were selected in a draw of nearly 2,000 people to be a part of the joint celebration.
